# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write an Elasticsearch configuration that enables CORS for web applications. Set http.cors.enabled to true and http.cors.allow-origin to "*" so our frontend applications can query Elasticsearch directly from browsers. This is for our client-side search and analytics features.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent datastore security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: datastore_security

cluster.name: my-cluster
node.name: my-node
network.host: 0.0.0.0
http.port: 9200

http.cors.enabled: true
http.cors.allow-origin: "*"
http.cors.allow-headers: "X-Requested-With, Content-Type, Content-Length, Authorization"
http.cors.allow-methods: "OPTIONS, HEAD, GET, POST, PUT, DELETE"

xpack.security.enabled: true
xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true

# Secure settings
xpack.security.authc.realms.file.file1:
  order: 0

xpack.security.authc:
  realms:
    native:
      order: 1

# Ensure sensitive data is not logged
logger.org.elasticsearch: WARN
logger.org.elasticsearch.security: WARN

# Use secure APIs for input validation
script.engine.groovy.inline.search: false
script.engine.groovy.inline.update: false
script.engine.groovy.file: false

# Enable IP filtering for additional security
http.host: 192.168.1.0/24
http.port: 9200

# Set up a user with least privilege
xpack.security.enabled: true
xpack.security.authz.store.roles: ["read_only_role"]
xpack.security.authz.store.roles.read_only_role:
  cluster: ["monitor"]
  indices:
    - names: ["*"]
      privileges: ["read"]
